33.play: What tributaries follow him to Rome,

35.play: You blocks, you stones, you worse than senseless things!

36.play: O you hard hearts, you cruel men of Rome,

37.play: Knew you not Pompey? Many a time and oft

38.play: Have you climb'd up to walls and battlements,

39.play: To towers and windows, yea, to chimney-tops,

40.play: Your infants in your arms, and there have sat

42.play: To see great Pompey pass the streets of Rome:

43.play: And when you saw his chariot but appear,

44.play: Have you not made an universal shout,

49.play: And do you now cull out a holiday?

51.play: That comes in triumph over Pompey's blood? Be gone!

52.play: Run to your houses, fall upon your knees,

53.play: Pray to the gods to intermit the plague

54.play: That needs must light on this ingratitude.
